title: ophrys tenthredinifera subsp. aprilia
intro: Ophrys tenthredinifera subsp. aprilia, also known as the Paphos bee orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ophrys tenthredinifera subsp. aprilia include its potential to treat wounds, infections, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for colds and fever. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use is not widely documented and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
